title: मेधा दक्षिणामूर्ति स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मेधा दक्षिणामूर्ति स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
मौनव्याख्याप्रकटितपरब्रह्मतत्त्वं युवानं
वर्षिष्ठान्ते वसदृषिगणैरावृतं ब्रह्मनिष्ठैः।
आचार्येन्द्रं करकलित चिन्मुद्रमानन्दमूर्तिं
स्वात्मारामं मुदितवदनं दक्षिणामूर्तिमीडे॥

ॐ नमो भगवते मेधादक्षिणामूर्तये।
मह्यं मेधां प्रज्ञां प्रयच्छ स्वाहा॥

मेधां मे वरुणो दधातु मेधामग्निस्तथैव च।
मेधां इन्द्रश्च वायुश्च मेधां धाता विदधातु मे॥१॥

या वेदेषु ऋचा चोक्ता या गन्धर्वेषु गीयते।
मेधा सा मयि संक्रामतु यथा धारणमक्षरम्॥२॥

मेधां मे देवः सविता मेधामाददातु सरस्वती।
मेधां मे अश्विनावुभावाधत्तां पुष्करस्रजा॥३॥

यत्साम ऋषयो विदुः तत्साम मयि संक्रामतु।
यदृषिगणा ज्ञानं विदुस्तज्ज्ञानं मयि संक्रामतु॥४॥

अप्सरासु च या मेधा गन्धर्वेषु च यन्मनः।
दैवीं मेधां सरस्वतीं तां मां मेधां ददातु मे॥५॥

मेधां मे सोमो राजा मेधां चन्द्रमसः पतिः।
सिद्धिमेधां च गोमेधां यज्ञमेधां ददातु मे॥६॥

यदनुभवमयं ज्ञानं गुरवः प्राहुरव्ययम्।
तद्विष्णोः परमं पदं सदा पश्यन्ति सूरयः॥७॥

ॐ अस्य श्री मेधादक्षिणामूर्तिस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य।
ब्रह्मा ऋषिः। गायत्री छन्दः। मेधादक्षिणामूर्तिर्देवता।
मेधाप्राप्त्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

चित्रं वटतरोर्मूले वृद्धाः शिष्या गुरुर्युवा।
गुरोस्तु मौनं व्याख्यानं शिष्यास्तु छिन्नसंशयाः॥८॥

ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

॥ इति श्री मेधा दक्षिणामूर्ति स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Medhā Dakṣiṇāmūrti Stotram ||

|| Dhyānam ||
Maunavyākhyāprakaṭitaparabrahmatattvaṁ yuvānaṁ
Varṣiṣṭhānte vasadṛṣigaṇairāvṛtaṁ brahmaniṣṭhaiḥ.
Āchāryendraṁ karakalita chinmudrāmānandamūrtiṁ
Svātmārāmaṁ muditavadanaṁ Dakṣiṇāmūrtimīḍe.

Om Namo Bhagavate Medhādakṣiṇāmūrtaye.
Mahyaṁ medhāṁ prajñāṁ prayachchha svāhā.

Medhāṁ me Varuṇo dadhātu medhāmAgnisthathaiva cha.
Medhāṁ Indraścha Vāyuścha medhāṁ Dhātā vidadhātu me.||1||

Yā Vedeṣu ṛchā choktā yā Gandharveṣu gīyate.
Medhā sā mayi saṁkrāmatu yathā dhāraṇamakṣaram.||2||

Medhāṁ me Devaḥ Savitā medhāmādadātu Sarasvatī.
Medhāṁ me Aśvināvubhāvādhattāṁ puṣkarasrajā.||3||

Yatsāma ṛṣayo viduḥ tatsāma mayi saṁkrāmatu.
Yadṛṣigaṇā jñānaṁ vidustajjñānaṁ mayi saṁkrāmatu.||4||

Apsarāsu cha yā medhā gandharveṣu cha yanmanaḥ.
Daivīṁ medhāṁ Sarasvatīṁ tāṁ māṁ medhāṁ dadātu me.||5||

Medhāṁ me Somo Rājā medhāṁ Chandramasaḥ Patiḥ.
Siddhimedhāṁ cha Gomedhāṁ Yajñamedhāṁ dadātu me.||6||

Yadanubhavamayaṁ jñānaṁ Guravaḥ prāhuravyayam.
TadViṣṇoḥ paramaṁ padaṁ sadā paśyanti sūrayaḥ.||7||

Om Asya Shrī Medhādakṣiṇāmūrtistotramanstrasya.
Brahmā ṛṣiḥ. Gāyatrī chhandaḥ. Medhādakṣiṇāmūrtirdevatā.
Medhāprāptyarthe jape viniyogaḥ.

Chitraṁ vaṭatarormūle vṛddhāḥ śiṣyā gururyuvā.
Gurosttu maunaṁ vyākhyānaṁ śiṣyāstu chhinnasaṁśayāḥ.||8||

Om Śāntiḥ Śāntiḥ Śāntiḥ.

|| Iti Shrī Medhā Dakṣiṇāmūrti Stotraṁ sampūrṇam ||
hindiMeaning: ध्यान: मौन व्याख्या से परब्रह्म तत्त्व को प्रकट करने वाले, युवा रूप में, ब्रह्मनिष्ठ वृद्ध ऋषिगणों से घिरे, हाथ में चिन्मुद्रा धारण किये, आनन्दमूर्ति, स्वात्माराम, प्रसन्न मुख वाले दक्षिणामूर्ति की मैं स्तुति करता हूँ। (1) वरुण मुझे मेधा दें, अग्नि मेधा दें, इन्द्र और वायु मेधा दें, धाता मुझे मेधा प्रदान करें। (2) जो मेधा वेदों में ऋचाओं द्वारा कही गई है, जो गन्धर्वों में गाई जाती है, वह मेधा मुझ में संक्रमित हो, जैसे अक्षर की धारणा होती है। (3) सविता देव मुझे मेधा दें, सरस्वती मेधा दें, पुष्करमाला धारी अश्विनीकुमार मुझे मेधा प्रदान करें। (4) जो साम ऋषि जानते हैं वह मुझ में आए, जो ज्ञान ऋषिगण जानते हैं वह मुझ में संक्रमित हो। (5-6) अप्सराओं और गन्धर्वों की दिव्य मेधा, सरस्वती रूप मेधा, सोम राजा की मेधा, सिद्धि-मेधा, गो-मेधा, यज्ञ-मेधा मुझे प्रदान हो। (8) वट वृक्ष के नीचे अद्भुत दृश्य है - शिष्य वृद्ध हैं, गुरु युवा हैं; गुरु का मौन ही व्याख्यान है और शिष्यों के सब संशय छिन्न हो गए हैं।
benefits: Invokes the blessing of medha (intellectual brilliance and retentive memory)
Invokes prajna (transcendental wisdom) through lord dakshinamurthy's grace
Invokes blessings of Varuna, Agni, Indra, Vayu, and Savita for intelligence
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of doubts and ignorance, as described in the final verse
Combines Vedic Medha Suktam with Dakshinamurthy worship for maximum effect
Ideal for students, scholars, and spiritual seekers
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Saraswati, Medha Dakshinamurthy Stotram (मेधा दक्षिणामूर्ति स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Attributed to Vedic Medha Suktam / Dakshinamurthy Tradition, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow Medha (intellectual brilliance and retentive memory) and remove all doubts and ignorance, as described in the final verse. It is also said to combine Vedic Medha Suktam with Dakshinamurthy worship for maximum effect. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Vasant Panchami and Guru Purnima. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 6 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Medha Dakshinamurthy Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Medha Dakshinamurthy Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful prayer for intellectual brilliance, memory, and transcendental wisdom.

seoFaqs.question: What is the textual basis of the Medha Dakshinamurthy Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The composition stands at the intersection of two scriptural streams. The first is the Medha Suktam, a Vedic hymn to Medha Devi, the goddess of intelligence, found in the Mahanarayana Upanishad section of the Krishna Yajurveda Taittiriya Aranyaka. The second is the Dakshinamurthy stotra tradition associated with Adi Shankaracharya's Dakshinamurthy Stotram, which presents Shiva as the silent guru facing south. The Medha Dakshinamurthy Stotram fuses these by invoking Dakshinamurthy as the conferrer of medha (intellectual capacity) using the Medha Suktam framing. The core mantra 'Om Namo Bhagavate Dakshinamurtaye Mahyam Medham Prajnam Prayaccha Svaha' makes this synthesis explicit.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Dakshinamurthy and why is he the deity of learning?
seoFaqs.answer: Dakshinamurthy is the youthful, south-facing form of Shiva who teaches the four eldest sages (Sanaka, Sanandana, Sanatana and Sanatkumara) in silence, conveying ultimate knowledge through chinmudra rather than words. Because he is the Adi Guru and embodies pure knowledge, the entire Hindu pedagogical tradition treats him as the patron deity of learning, with his image installed in the southern wall of every Shiva temple. The Medha Dakshinamurthy Stotram extends this guru identity by specifically asking him to grant medha (retention), prajna (discernment) and dhi (intellect), making it the canonical hymn for students, scholars and aspirants of brahma-vidya.

seoFaqs.question: What does the central Medha Dakshinamurthy mantra mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The mantra 'Om Namo Bhagavate Dakshinamurtaye Mahyam Medham Prajnam Prayaccha Svaha' translates as 'Om, salutations to the Divine Lord Dakshinamurthy. Please bestow upon me medha and prajna, svaha.' Medha denotes the retentive faculty of the intellect, the capacity to absorb and remember; prajna denotes the discriminative faculty, the ability to analyse and apply. The svaha ending marks it as a Tantric yajna mantra suitable for homa, japa and abhisheka use. Traditional teachers prescribe 108 repetitions daily during a sankalpa-bound period (commonly 41 or 48 days), often paired with the longer Medha Dakshinamurthy Stotram and the Medha Suktam for full effect.

seoFaqs.question: How is the Medha Dakshinamurthy Stotram used during Vidyarambham and Akshara Abhyasam?
seoFaqs.answer: Vidyarambham (the formal initiation of a child into letters), traditionally observed in Kerala on Vijayadashami and across South India on Saraswati Puja and Akshara Abhyasam days, prescribes the Medha Dakshinamurthy mantra and stotram as part of the ritual. The child is seated facing east, the priest or guru writes the syllable 'Om' or 'Hari Sri Ganapataye Namah' on the child's tongue with gold and on a tray of rice while reciting the mantra. The stotram is chanted to invoke Dakshinamurthy as the first guru of the child's learning journey. Many South Indian schools also recite the stotram before annual examinations as a tradition of seeking medha-prasada.

seoFaqs.question: How does the Medha Dakshinamurthy Stotram differ from Shankaracharya's main Dakshinamurthy Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Adi Shankaracharya's Dakshinamurthy Stotram, beginning 'Vishvam darpana drishyamana', is a ten-verse Advaita Vedanta composition expounding the doctrine that the world is a reflection in the mirror of consciousness and that the Self alone is real. It is a jnana-stotram aimed at moksha. The Medha Dakshinamurthy Stotram, by contrast, is a vidya-prarthana, a petition for intellectual capacity, drawing more from the Tantric Medha Suktam tradition than from pure Advaita. The two are complementary rather than identical: one cultivates the ground of intellect through medha-prarthana, the other points the matured intellect to non-dual realisation. Many traditional schools recite both, the Medha version daily and the Advaita version during deeper study.
